title: Oscars 2026: The best jokes from Conan O’Brien’s monologue
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien hosted the Oscars for the second consecutive year on March 15, 2026, at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles.
O'Brien's monologue included jokes about Netflix, Timothee Chalamet, and the future of the Oscars airing on YouTube.
Jimmy Kimmel joined as a guest, taking shots at CBS while presenting the Best Documentary Feature and Short categories.

title: Fundraiser: Community remembers Danvers mother, nurse killed in her home
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Janet Swallow, 68, a nurse and mother from Danvers, was allegedly killed by 18-year-old Anthony DeMayo in her home.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised nearly $50,000 to support Swallow's family, with a goal of reaching $65,000.
Anthony DeMayo, student at Bishop Fenwick High School, faces murder charges after allegedly admitting to killing Swallow.

title: Northeastern women’s hockey advances to Frozen Four after defeating Minnesota
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Northeastern women's hockey defeated Minnesota 4-2 to advance to the Frozen Four.
Sophomore goalie Lisa Jönsson made 45 saves, helping secure Northeastern's win.
Northeastern will face No. 1 Ohio State in the Frozen Four hosted by Penn State.

title: Bomb threat reportedly halts plane at Logan ‘for hours’ before being deemed ‘not credible’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A bomb threat delayed a JetBlue flight at Boston Logan Airport for hours before being deemed not credible by authorities.
The threat, called in by an unidentified person, targeted a plane bound for Vero Beach, Florida, State Police confirmed.
FBI agents and bomb-sniffing dogs searched the plane; passengers' luggage was also inspected before they could leave.

title: 17 arrested during St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seventeen people were arrested during the South Boston St. Patrick's Day parade for infractions related to public drinking.
Boston police could not disclose specific charges or reports regarding the arrests at the parade attended by nearly a million people.
Complaints about underage drinking and public indecency were reported to 311, prompting police response during the parade.

title: 2026 Oscars: Full list of winners (updating live)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'One Battle After Another' won six Oscars, including Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Adapted Screenplay at the 2026 Academy Awards.
'Sinners' earned four Oscars with wins for Best Actor, Best Original Screenplay, Best Cinematography, and Best Score.
Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor for 'Sinners,' expressing gratitude to influential figures like Sidney Poitier and Denzel Washington.

title: Oscars red carpet: See the best Academy Awards fashion
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The 2026 Oscars red carpet event took place at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, featuring top Hollywood fashion.
Conan O'Brien, hosting the Oscars again, attended with Liza Powel O'Brien, remaining silent about his opening monologue.
A-listers such as Kate Hudson, Timothee Chalamet, and Elle Fanning were among the stars showcasing fashion on the red carpet.

title: All the way from Indonesia, she’s running the Boston Marathon for people with visual impairments
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas from Jakarta, Indonesia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support people with visual impairments.
Miraningtyas, who has pterygium, emphasizes the importance of accessible eye care and uses her marathon to raise awareness.
She plans to undergo surgery on her second eye post-marathon, highlighting surgery costs as a barrier for many.

title: 5 takeaways from the Revolution’s 6-1 dismantling of FC Cincinnati
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution matched a club record with six goals in a 6-1 victory over FC Cincinnati.
Revolution center back Brayan Ceballos scored twice from corner kicks, while Luca Langoni provided three assists.
Diego Fagundez returned to the Revolution, assisting a goal in the final minutes of the match.

title: Man dies after kayak overturns off Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 22-year-old man died after his kayak overturned in Wellfleet, Cape Cod, on Saturday evening.
Emergency crews, including a Coast Guard helicopter, conducted a search for nearly two hours before finding him.
The man was taken to Cape Cod Hospital in critical condition but was pronounced dead upon arrival.
